draft.introduction: The yogi who sees the Self in all beings and all beings in the Self never loses sight of God, and God never loses sight of the yogi. This is yoga as mutual vision.

translation.text: He who is established in yoga sees the Self abiding in all beings and all beings in the Self; he sees the same everywhere. He who sees me everywhere and sees everything in me—I am never lost to him, nor is he ever lost to me.
translation.original_text: sarva-bhūta-sthaṃ ātmānaṃ sarva-bhūtāni cātmani | īkṣate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśanaḥ

translator_notes.note: The phrase “I am never lost to him nor is he ever lost to me” describes a reciprocal divine-human relationship. This is not absorption but mutual seeing—closer to Christian beatific vision than to Advaitic dissolution.
